title: Accessibility (WCAG 2.2 AA) + plain-language pass + translation-readiness extraction
context: For Oncology-Data-Literacy to reach non-expert and non-English audiences, the courseware must meet WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility standards, pass a plain-language review, and be translation-ready (structured so that statistical claims and citations remain intact during translation). Accessibility and plain-language work are scoped to tooling/platform (low risk tier); the patient-advocate reviewer assists on the plain-language pass since they represent the beneficiary audience. Translation-readiness is about structure, not authoring — it ensures strings are extractable and citations remain linked through translation.
objective: Run an accessibility pass (WCAG 2.2 AA), a plain-language pass, and translation-readiness extraction across all three courses (Readings Rates, Reading Survival, Reading Screening Evidence). Wire automated a11y checks into CI. Produce a plain-language review report identifying terms that need simplification. Extract translation-ready strings in a structure that keeps statistical claims keyed to their citations (so per-language expert review of translations can verify claim accuracy).
acceptanceCriteria: Automated WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility checks are wired into CI and passing on all published course modules
Manual a11y audit (or detailed report from automated tooling) confirms no known AA failures remain in the published courseware
Plain-language pass produces a report identifying terms simplified or flagged for further simplification; readability scoring meets the target for the intended audience
Translation-readiness: all course strings are extractable in a structured format (e.g., a standard i18n format) that keeps statistical claims keyed to their register citations
Patient-advocate reviewer confirms the plain-language pass output on patient-facing modules
Maintainer-reviewed PR with CI green (a11y checks passing) before merge
output: A merged PR adding WCAG 2.2 AA checks to CI (passing), a plain-language review report, and translation-ready string extraction — ensuring the courseware is accessible to and adaptable for non-expert and non-English audiences.
